name: Carrd
price_basis: Pro Standard at $19 per year, normalized monthly, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: KEEP: $19 a year buys ten custom-domain sites. Rebuilding them to save pocket change is cosplay accounting.
what_zo_builds: Zo can build the same landing pages, forms, embeds, analytics, and metadata. That does not make migration a good use of time when Carrd prices ten sites below two dollars a month.
dont_kill_if: Keep it by default. Kill only when you already need server-side logic, workspace-owned data, or a Zo integration Carrd cannot run. Complexity, not price, is the reason to move.
build_brief: Goal: decide whether one Carrd site has outgrown Carrd. Inventory its forms, embeds, analytics, domains, redirects, and update frequency. If it only needs static content, stop and KEEP Carrd. If it needs server logic or private workspace data, recreate that one site on Zo and preserve its URLs and metadata. Done when the decision is written down and no site moves merely to save $19 a year.
